author: Pam Baker
iteration_root_cause: Assembler leaked article body before <html> tag due to malformed self-documenting comment in skeleton.html (inner '-->' closed outer comment prematurely). Fixed by stripping doctype-to-<html> preamble before substitution.

spans.notes: ingest-preflight.ps1 extracted 109 HTML splits + ~hundreds of PNGs from EPUB to TEMP
17 chapters processed, 75,565 output words (86.1% verbatim), 0% bridges, 1,156 paragraphs dropped with reasons
584/1621 anchors verified (full sweep on concept chapters 3/5/11/15, 10% sample elsewhere). 0 violations. Coverage band all-pass.
Navigation=hybrid (6 themes), 38 diagrams (29 mermaid + 9 html_table), 17 chapter heroes, 12 pull-quotes. Style=photographic_realism, accent=#1e5fa8 (ink blue), book_seed=1484186380
Generated 10 comprehension questions covering chapters 1, 2, 3, 7, 10, 12, 13. Weighted toward conceptual spine and named entities.
Output had pre-doctype dump — 2088 lines leaked before <html> tag because the skeleton's self-documenting comment contained literal example placeholders and was malformed HTML.
Caught the Assembler defect. Comprehension 10/10 confirms content is recoverable; visual_pleasure + books_finished_rate fail due to envelope, not content.
Strip-preamble fix applied; output is clean single-document HTML (677 KB, 2333 lines, one each of doctype/html/body/article).
Terminal gate cleared (books_finished_rate=4). Lowest dim visual_pleasure reflects the 0% bridge ratio — text is a raw anchor stack with no Editor connective prose.
